purpose: add_rule_from_csv's malformed arms: a missing file raises the loader's own FileNotFoundError ('CSV file not found: <path>'); an empty rule_text cell raises ValueError with the doubled row prefix ("Row 1: Failed to parse rule - Row 1: Missing required 'rule_text'" — the inner message is re-wrapped at pyreason.py:851); a file-local duplicate name raises at row 2 AFTER loading row 1 (partial load pinned by the closing fingerprint: [same_name] only); and an unquoted comma inside rule_text makes pandas' tokenizer fail wholesale (ValueError 'Error reading CSV file ...: Error tokenizing data ...'), loading nothing from that file — the row-wise vs whole-file failure asymmetry pinned in one case.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_rule_from_csv malformed-missing-file/malformed-missing-rule_text/malformed-duplicate-name/malformed-unreadable; pyreason.py:784-866 at the pin; exception text screened live 2026-07-07
